title: Tracing Garbage Collection and Conservative Limits · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar tracing garbage collection and conservative limits decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for small mark-and-sweep collector model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.
Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Reachability Roots
steps.instruction:
Order the Reachability Roots evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Reachability Roots.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Reachability Roots assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Reachability Roots, keep Reachability Roots connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 3323. Produce bounded C evidence to mark reachable objects and count only unreachable sweep candidates; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
